raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/XXXX I was charged {$5500.00} to my American Express Credit card by XXXX XXXX. We attempted to contact the window services with general questions on or about XX/XX/XXXX. The XXXX became hostile, and immediately stated we could not cancel anything. We asked how products could be ordered without an executed contract or permits being pulled. On or about XX/XX/XXXX I contacted American Express to dispute the charge as no services have been rendered and I have not signed any contract. On XX/XX/XXXX American Express closed the dispute and said I was responsible for the charge due to the contractors cancellation policy. On or about XX/XX/XXXX I contacted American Express again to inquire how I could be held to a contract I never received or signed by the vendor. I informed them there was no valid contract as per the NJ Consumer Fraud Protection Act. American Express again stated they would reopen the dispute as a no valid contract ( and fraud I believe ). On XX/XX/XXXX American Express again closed my dispute citing the contractors cancellation policy. I immediately contacted American Empress via phone and was told American Express is bound to the contractors policies per FTC law and that state laws have no bearing. As of now I am still being charged {$5500.00} for services I never agreed to and have not received. Any assistance would be greatly appreciated.
